species.citation: (Prance) Sothers & Prance , Blumea 59 (2014) 104; ≡ Licania palawanensis Prance , Brittonia 31 (1979) 94 ; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1989) 646; Type: Mendoza & Espirita PNH91305 (BM, holo; K, L, PNH, iso), Palawan: Queza.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. PALAWAN, SIBUYAN, TAMLANGON.
species.notes: Forest on ultramafics, including seashore forests. 0-300m.

species.citation: Korth. , Ned. Kruidk. Arch. 3 (1854) 384 ; Merrill, PJS 10c (1915) 307; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 236; ≡ Licania splendens (Korth.) Prance , Fl. Neotrop. Monogr. 9 (1972) 172 ; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1989) 646; Type: from Borneo. = Parinari philippinensis Elmer , LPB 10 (1939) 3809, nom. inval.
species.distribution: Borneo, Java, Malay Peninsula, Philippines, Sumatra, Thailand. BUSUANGA, LUZON: Aurora, Isabela, MINDANAO: Davao Oriental, Surigao, Surigao del Norte, PALAWAN, PANAY, SAMAR, SIBUYAN, TAWI-TAWI.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 5 October 2025

species.citation: (Jack) Kosterm. , Reinwardtia 7 (1969) 422 ; Prance, Gard. Bull. Singapore 71 (2019) 87; ≡ Petrocarya excelsa Jack , Malayan Misc. 2 (1822) 66; Type: from Sumatra. ssp. excelsa. = Parinari villamilii Merr., PJS 10c (1915) 308, as 'Parinarium villamilii'; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 236. Distribution: Borneo, Java, Malay Peninsula, Philippines, Sumatra. LUZON: Camarines, MINDANAO: Zamboanga. Notes: Lowland forests. 0-750m. Native. ssp. racemosa (Raf.) Prance, Gard. Bull. Singapore 71 (2019) 88; ≡ Atuna racemosa ssp. racemosa; ≡ Atuna racemosa Raf., Sylva Tellur. (1838) 153; Kosterm., Reinwardtia 7 (1969) 422; Prance, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1989) 669; Cañeda et al., Davao Research J. 16 (2025) 91; Type: from Indonesia. = Parinari curranii Merr., PJS 4c (1909) 264, as 'Parinarium curranii'; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 236. = Parinari racemosa Merr., Publ. Bur. Sci. Gov. Lab. 17 (1904) 19, nom. illeg., as 'Parinarium racemosum'; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 236. = Parinari scabra Hassk., Flora 27 (1844) 585, as 'Parinarium scabrum'; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 236. = Parinari laurina A.Gray, U.S. Expl. Exped., Phan. (1854) 490, as 'Parinarium laurinum'; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 236. = Parinari mindanaensis Perkins, Fragm. Fl. Philipp. (1904) 119, as 'Parinarium mindanaense'; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 236. = Parinari glaberrima (Hassk.) Hassk., Flora 27 (1844) 583, as 'Parinarium glaberrimum'; Tijdschr. Nat. Gesch. 10 (1843) 147, nom. inval., nom. nud.; Cat. Hort. Bogor. (1844) 269, nom. inval., nom. nud.; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 236; ≡ Cyclandrophora glaberrima Hassk., Flora 25(Beibl.) (1842) 47. Distribution: Borneo, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, New Guinea, Pacific Ocean, Philippines, Singapore, Solomon Isls, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand. CAMIGUIN (photos), LUZON: Albay, Camarines, MASBATE, MINDANAO: Davao Occidental, PALAWAN, SIBUYAN. Notes: Lowland and medium elevation forests. 0-600m. Conservation status: Least Concern (IUCN 3.1). Native.
species.distribution: Borneo, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, New Guinea, Pacific Ocean, Philippines, Singapore, Solomon Isls, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand. CAMIGUIN (photos), LUZON: Albay, Camarines, MASBATE, MINDANAO: Davao Occidental, Davao del Sur (photos), Lanao del Norte (photos), Zamboanga, MINDORO, PALAWAN, PANAY (photos), SIBUYAN.

species.citation: Blume , Bijdr. fl. Ned. Ind. (1825) 89 ; Prance, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1989) 673; Romeroso et al., J. Asia-Pacific Biodiv. 17 (2024) 10; ≡ Parinari corymbosa (Blume) Miq. , Fl. Ned. Ind. 1(1) (1855) 356, as 'Parinarium corymbosum' ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 235. = Grymania salicifolia C.Presl , Abh. Königl. Böhm. Ges. Wiss., 5th Ser. 6 (1851) 553. = Parinari griffithiana Benth. , Niger Fl. (1849) 334.
species.distribution: Australia, Borneo, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, New Guinea, Pacific Ocean, Philippines, Solomon Isls, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand. BILIRAN, GUIMARAS, HOMONHON, LUZON: Abra, Albay, Aurora, Bataan, Cagayan, Camarines, Camarines Sur, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, Isabela, Laguna, Nueva Ecija, Pangasinan, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, Tarlac, Zambales, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Davao Oriental, Davao del Sur, Lanao, Misamis Oriental (photos), Zamboanga Sibugay, MINDORO, PALAUI (photos), PALAWAN, PANAY, PANGULASIAN, ROMBLON, SAMAR, SIBUYAN, TABLAS.
species.notes: N Luzon to Palawan and Mindanao. Lowland and medium elevation forests. 0-600m.

species.citation: Kosterm. , New Crit. Malaysian Pl. 3 (1955) 25 ; Reinwardtia 7 (1965) 159; Prance, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1989) 656.

(Korth.) Blume , Mélang. Bot. 2 (1855) 10 ; Miq., Fl. Ind. Bat. 11 (1855) 354; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 236; Prance, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1989) 663; ≡ Lepidocarpa costata Korth. , Ned. Kruidk. Arch. 3 (1855) 387. ssp. costata. Distribution: Borneo, Malay Peninsula, Philippines, Sumatra. CULION, MINDANAO: Davao, Surigao, SAMAR. Notes: Lowland forests, hillsides, ridges. 0-300m. Native. ssp. rubiginosa (Ridl.) Prance, Brittonia 39 (1987) 368; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1989) 663; ≡ Parinari costata var. rubiginosa Ridl., J. Fed. Malay States Mus. 6 (1915) 143. = Parinari bicolor Merr., PJS 10c (1915) 309; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 235; Kosterm., Reinwardtia 7 (1965) 172. Distribution: Borneo, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Philippines. BUCAS GRANDE, HOMONHON (photos), MINDANAO: Surigao, SAMAR. Notes: Note by L.L.Co: check relationship of this subspecies with the typical subspecies. From the type images at hand, I think there’s no Parinari costata ssp. costata in the Philippines. Lowland forests and open slopes. (?-)750-1500m. Native.
species.distribution: Borneo, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Philippines, Sumatra. BUCAS GRANDE, CULION, HOMONHON (photos), MINDANAO: Davao, Davao Oriental (photos), Surigao, MINDORO, SAMAR.
